title: 11 Best Herbal Teas For Oily Hair
intro: Herbal teas for Oily hair are a natural remedy that involves the use of various plant-based teas to help regulate oil production and balance the scalp's natural pH. These teas can treat oily hair by reducing inflammation and soothing the scalp, thereby slowing down oil production. Examples of herbal teas that are effective in treating oily hair include peppermint tea, which cools and calms the scalp, rosemary tea, which improves circulation and reduces dandruff, and lavender tea, which has antiseptic and antifungal properties to control scalp infections. Additionally, other herbal teas such as green tea, chamomile tea, and sage tea can also be used to treat oily hair due to their ability to balance oil production, reduce inflammation, and promote a healthy scalp.

preparations.herb_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis teas, derived from the leaves of the rosemary plant, have been traditionally used to treat oily hair and scalp issues. The antiseptic and astringent properties of this herbal preparation help to control oil production and reduce the appearance of oily hair. The bioactive constituents, including camphor, bornel, and rosmarinic acid, help to balance the scalp's natural pH and reduce inflammation, thereby treating oily hair. Regular consumption of Rosmarinus officinalis teas has been found to provide numerous benefits, including reduced oiliness, improved scalp health, and a refreshing, clean scalp.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Rosmarinus officinalis leaves or 1 teaspoon of dried leaves.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add the Rosmarinus officinalis leaves to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and let it cool before using as a final rinse for oily hair.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Rosmarinus officinalis teas can be an effective treatment for oily hair, but it may also cause side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset in some individuals due to its high content of essential oils. To minimize the risk of these side effects, it's essential to start with a low dose, gradually increase the amount as needed, and avoid consuming the tea on an empty stomach, especially for those with sensitive stomachs or allergies to rosemary.
preparation_usage: Mix 2 teaspoons of Rosmarinus officinalis tea with 1 cup of water.
Shampoo your hair as usual, then pour the tea mixture over it.
Leave the tea on for 5-10 minutes, then rinse thoroughly.
Use the tea 1-2 times a week for best results on oily hair.
Drink 1 cup of the tea as a supplement to improve scalp health.

preparation_amazon.title: Palm Beach Medicinal Herbs - Rosemary Tea - Pure Herbal Tea Series, 30ct

preparations.herb_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas have been traditionally used to treat oily hair, thanks to their unique properties that help to balance and regulate the scalp's oil production. The antiseptic and antifungal properties of this herbal preparation help to control the growth of bacteria and fungi that can contribute to oily hair, while its astringent properties help to tighten the scalp and reduce oil secretion. The bioactive constituents of Lavandula angustifolia teas, including linalool and linalyl acetate, have been shown to have a positive effect on reducing sebum production and promoting a healthy scalp. Regular consumption of Lavandula angustifolia teas may help to alleviate oily hair by promoting a balanced scalp and reducing the appearance of excessive oiliness.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers. You can use fresh flowers for a more potent tea.
Combine 1 cup of boiling water with 2 tablespoons of Lavandula angustifolia flowers in a heat-resistant cup.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Let the tea cool down to room temperature. You can speed up the process by placing the cup in an ice bath.
Once cooled, use the Lavandula angustifolia tea as a final rinse after shampooing your oily hair to help balance its natural pH.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Lavandula angustifolia teas can be used to treat oily hair by balancing the scalp's oil production, but it may cause side effects such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, or an increase in dandruff due to its drying properties. To use Lavandula angustifolia teas safely, it's essential to start with small amounts, gradually increase the concentration, and avoid using it on damaged or irritated scalps, as well as to rinse the hair thoroughly after use to prevent any potential dryness.
preparation_usage: Wash hair with a gentle shampoo as usual.
Use 1 teaspoon of Lavandula angustifolia tea as a final rinse.
Soak hair in the tea for 5-7 minutes.
Repeat use 1-2 times a week for optimal results.
Combine with a balanced diet and regular hair care.

preparations.herb_desc: Thymus vulgaris teas, derived from the herb thyme, have been traditionally used to treat oily hair ailments due to their astringent, antiseptic, and antifungal properties. The bioactive constituents of thyme, including thymol and carvacrol, help to control oil production, reduce bacterial growth, and balance the scalp's pH, thereby treating oily hair. By regulating the scalp's oil production and reducing inflammation, thymus vulgaris teas help to prevent greasiness, itchiness, and dandruff associated with oily hair. The benefits of using thymus vulgaris teas for oily hair treatment include a reduction in oiliness, a healthier scalp, and a fresher, cleaner appearance.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 1 tablespoon of dried Thymus vulgaris leaves.
Steep the leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup to remove the leaves.
Let the tea cool down to room temperature.
Use the tea as a final rinse after shampooing your hair to help control oiliness.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Current topics in medicinal chemistry", Thymus vulgaris teas for oily hair may be beneficial as thyme has been reported to demonstrate anti-dandruff activity by disrupting the microbial growth associated with dandruff formation, potentially helping to alleviate scalp conditions that contribute to oily hair.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Thymus vulgaris teas can be used to treat oily hair, but its prolonged use may cause skin irritation, allergic reactions, and an increased heart rate in some individuals. To minimize potential side effects, it is recommended to start with low doses and monitor your body's response, avoid using it for extended periods, and be cautious when combining it with other medications or substances that may interact with its active compounds.
preparation_usage: Wash hair with shampoo as usual, 1-2 times a week.
Mix 1 teaspoon of Thymus vulgaris tea with 1 cup of water.
Use the mixture as a final rinse after shampooing.
Leave it on for 5 minutes before washing off with cool water.
Repeat the process 2-3 times a week for best results.

preparations.herb_desc: Cymbopogon citratus teas, also known as lemongrass tea, have been traditionally used to treat oily hair ailments due to its natural antiseptic and astringent properties. The tea helps to treat oily hair by reducing sebum production, promoting a healthy scalp, and regulating oil secretion, thereby balancing the hair's natural oil levels. The bioactive constituents of lemongrass tea, including citral, geraniol, and limonene, work synergistically to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi that can contribute to oily hair, while also possessing antioxidant properties that help to soothe and calm the scalp. The benefits of using Cymbopogon citratus teas to treat oily hair include a reduction in oiliness, a balanced scalp, and a healthy-looking hair with a natural shine.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Cymbopogon citratus leaves or 1 teaspoon of dried leaves.
Bring 1 cup of water to a boil in a pot.
Add the Cymbopogon citratus leaves to the boiling water and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and let it cool down. Store it in the fridge for up to 24 hours.
To use the tea, pour 1 cup of it over your hair after shampooing and let it sit for 15-30 minutes before rinsing.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cymbopogon citratus teas can be beneficial in treating oily hair due to its natural astringent properties, but potential side effects may include skin irritation, allergic reactions, and an increased risk of interaction with other medications. To minimize risks, individuals should use Cymbopogon citratus teas in moderation, avoid applying it directly on the scalp, and be cautious of any unusual or severe reactions, such as dizziness or rapid heartbeat.
preparation_usage: Wash your hair with shampoo as usual.
Mix 1 tablespoon of Cymbopogon citratus tea with 1 cup of water.
Apply the solution as a final rinse after shampooing.
Leave it on for 5-10 minutes, then rinse with warm water.
Repeat once a week for best results, 3-4 times a month as maintenance.

preparations.herb_desc: Salvia officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat oily hair ailments due to their antiseptic and antifungal properties, which help to control the growth of bacteria and fungi that contribute to excessive oil production. These teas work by regulating the scalp's natural oil production, reducing the greasiness and flakiness associated with oily hair. The bioactive constituents of Salvia officinalis, including camphor, borneol, and thymol, have been found to possess antiseptic and antifungal properties that help to treat oily hair. Regular consumption of Salvia officinalis teas has been reported to help control oily hair, promote a healthy scalp, and reduce the appearance of greasiness and flakiness.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Salvia officinalis leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Add 2 tablespoons of honey to the boiling water and mix well.
Add the fresh Salvia officinalis leaves to the honey-water mixture.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes and then strain it.
Drink the tea 1-2 times a week to help reduce oiliness in your hair.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Salvia officinalis teas can be used to treat oily hair due to its antiseptic and antifungal properties, but possible side effects may include skin irritation, allergic reactions, or digestive issues in some individuals. To minimize potential risks, it is essential to start with small amounts and monitor your body's response, avoid using it on broken skin, and combine it with a balanced diet and regular hair care routine.
preparation_usage: Wash hair with shampoo and towel-dry for best results.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried Salvia officinalis in 1 cup of hot water.
Let the tea cool down and use as final rinse after shampooing.
Use Salvia officinalis tea as a final rinse once or twice a week.
Continue using for 2-3 months to see noticeable reduction in oiliness.

preparations.herb_desc: Saponaria officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat oily hair ailments, which is attributed to their astringent and antiseptic properties. This herbal preparation helps to treat oily hair by balancing the scalp's natural oil production, thereby reducing excess oil and promoting a healthier scalp environment. The bioactive constituents of Saponaria officinalis, including saponins and flavonoids, are responsible for its therapeutic effects, which help to regulate the scalp's sebaceous glands and reduce dandruff and itchiness. The benefits of using Saponaria officinalis teas to treat oily hair include a reduction in oiliness, improved scalp health, and a decrease in dandruff and itchiness, making it a natural and effective remedy for this common issue.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Saponaria officinalis flowers.
Combine the flowers with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain it through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve.
Measure 1 tablespoon of apple cider vinegar and mix it with 1 cup of the cooled tea.
Use the tea as a final rinse after shampooing your hair to help control oiliness.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Saponaria officinalis teas can be used to treat oily hair by balancing the scalp's natural oils and reducing its sebum production, but it may cause some side effects such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and dryness of the scalp and hair. To minimize these side effects, it is recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase it, use a mild shampoo, and avoid combining Saponaria officinalis teas with other hair care products that contain harsh ingredients or fragrances.
preparation_usage: Wash hair with regular shampoo before using Saponaria officinalis tea.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried Saponaria officinalis in 1 cup of hot water for 5-7 minutes.
Let the tea cool down to lukewarm temperature.
Use the cooled tea as final rinse after shampooing, 1-2 times a week.
Mix 1/2 cup of the tea with 1 cup of water and use it as final rinse for oily hair.

preparations.herb_desc: Achillea millefolium teas have been used to treat oily hair due to their astringent and antiseptic properties, which help to balance the scalp's natural pH and reduce oil production. This herbal preparation also contains bioactive constituents such as sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids that work together to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi that contribute to oily hair. The tea's astringent properties help to tighten the hair follicles and reduce excess sebum, resulting in a healthier and more balanced scalp. Regular consumption of Achillea millefolium teas may also help to reduce dandruff, itchiness, and flakiness associated with oily hair, promoting overall scalp health and well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh Achillea millefolium leaves and flowers.
Chop the leaves and flowers into small pieces.
Steep 2 tablespoons of the chopped mixture in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a separate container. Discard the solids.
Let the tea cool, then use it as a final rinse after shampooing your hair.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Achillea millefolium teas can be beneficial in treating oily hair due to its ability to regulate sebum production and balance the scalp's natural pH, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. Precautions should be taken when using this herbal preparation, including starting with a small dose, avoiding consumption if pregnant or breastfeeding, and monitoring for signs of interaction with other medications.
preparation_usage: Wash hair with shampoo to remove dirt and oils.
Make a tea by steeping 1 tablespoon of dried Achillea millefolium in 1 cup of boiling water.
Strain tea and let it cool to room temperature.
Use cooled tea as a final rinse after shampooing, 2-3 times a week.
Repeat tea rinse for 2-4 weeks to see noticeable reduction in oiliness.

preparations.herb_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia teas, derived from the leaves of the tea tree, have been traditionally used to treat oily hair ailments due to their potent antimicrobial and antifungal properties. The terpenes and flavonoids present in these teas, particularly cineole and leucadendron, help to control the growth of bacteria and fungi that contribute to oily scalp conditions. By regulating the scalp's natural oil production and reducing inflammation, Melaleuca alternifolia teas help to alleviate symptoms of oily hair, such as dandruff and itchiness. The benefits of using Melaleuca alternifolia teas for oily hair treatment include a balanced scalp pH, reduced oil production, and a decrease in the risk of scalp infections.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water and 1 teaspoon of Melaleuca alternifolia leaves.
Heat the water in a pot or kettle until it starts boiling.
Add the Melaleuca alternifolia leaves to the boiling water and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the leaves.
Use the tea as a final rinse after shampooing your hair to help control oiliness.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Melaleuca alternifolia teas can be beneficial in treating oily hair, but possible side effects may include skin irritation, allergic reactions, and an increased risk of interaction with other medications due to the tea's active compounds, such as cineole. To minimize potential side effects, it is essential to start with a low dose, gradually increase the amount as needed, and avoid using the tea for extended periods, as excessive consumption may lead to the buildup of toxic compounds in the body.
preparation_usage: Wash hair with a gentle shampoo as usual, 2-3 times a week.
Rinse hair with 1 tablespoon of Melaleuca alternifolia tea as a final rinse.
Leave the tea on hair for 1-2 minutes before rinsing with cold water.
Repeat the tea rinse 1-2 times a week for optimal results.
Use 1-2 cups of Melaleuca alternifolia tea as a final rinse, if needed.

preparations.herb_desc: Eucalyptus globulus teas have been traditionally used to help treat oily hair by reducing excess sebum production and balancing the scalp's natural pH levels. The antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties of this herbal preparation help to eliminate bacteria and soothe irritated scalp, thereby reducing dandruff and itchiness associated with oily hair. Eucalyptus globulus teas contain bioactive constituents such as tannins, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi that contribute to oily hair. Regular consumption of Eucalyptus globulus teas can help to regulate the scalp's natural oil production, leading to a healthier and more balanced scalp, and ultimately, reducing oily hair.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Eucalyptus globulus leaves, 2 cups of boiling water, and a strainer.
Crush the Eucalyptus leaves with a spoon to release their oils and flavor.
Steep the crushed leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a strainer into a cup or teapot, and discard the leaves.
Drink the Eucalyptus tea 2-3 times a week to help control oily hair, and rinse your hair with the tea as a final rinse after shampooing.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Eucalyptus globulus teas can be used to treat oily hair, but possible side effects may include skin irritation, allergic reactions, and an increased heart rate due to the presence of eucalyptol, a compound that can cause an allergic response in some individuals. To use Eucalyptus globulus teas safely, it's essential to start with a small amount and gradually increase the dose, and avoid using it on broken or irritated skin, as well as during pregnancy or breastfeeding due to its potential effects on the reproductive system.
preparation_usage: Wash hair with shampoo as usual.
Mix 1 cup of Eucalyptus globulus tea with 1 cup of water.
Use the tea mixture as a final rinse for your hair.
Repeat the process 1-2 times a week for best results.
Leave the tea mixture on for 5-10 minutes before rinsing.

preparations.herb_desc: Melissa officinalis teas, also known as lemon balm teas, have been traditionally used to treat oily hair ailments due to their astringent and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Melissa officinalis, such as rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid, help to reduce the secretion of sebum, thereby controlling oily hair. The tea helps to treat oily hair by balancing the scalp's natural pH and reducing the growth of bacteria that can contribute to an oily scalp, resulting in healthier and more manageable hair. Regular consumption of Melissa officinalis teas has been shown to provide long-term benefits in managing oily hair, including reduced greasiness and a balanced scalp.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Melissa officinalis leaves or 1 teaspoon of dried leaves.
Combine the leaves with 1 cup of boiling water in a teapot.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid.
Add 1 tablespoon of lemon juice to the tea for extra benefits.
Use the cooled tea as a final rinse after shampooing your hair to help control oiliness.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Melissa officinalis teas can be effective in treating oily hair, but they may also cause side effects such as dizziness, headaches, and allergic reactions in some individuals, particularly those with sensitive skin or allergies. To use Melissa officinalis teas safely, it is essential to start with a low dose, gradually increase the amount as needed, and avoid consuming them in excessive quantities, as this may lead to an imbalance in the body's natural oil production.
preparation_usage: Use 2 tablespoons of Melissa officinalis dried leaves per cup of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain it.
Drink 1 cup of Melissa officinalis tea twice a day.
Leave the tea on your hair for 30 minutes to an hour before washing.
Shampoo and condition your hair as usual after using the tea treatment.

preparations.herb_desc: Artemisia absinthium teas have been traditionally used to treat oily hair, a common issue many people face. This herbal preparation is beneficial in treating oily hair due to its astringent, antiseptic, and antifungal properties, which help to regulate the scalp's natural oil production and prevent the growth of fungi that can contribute to oily hair. Artemisia absinthium teas contain bioactive constituents such as thujone, bornyl acetate, and borneol, which have been found to have a drying effect on the scalp, reducing oiliness and promoting a healthy scalp environment. The regular consumption of Artemisia absinthium teas can help to alleviate oily hair, promoting a balanced scalp and healthy-looking hair.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 2-3 tablespoons of dried Artemisia absinthium leaves.
Steep the leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Strain the liquid using a fine-mesh sieve.
Let the tea cool down. You can speed up the process by placing the tea in the refrigerator.
Once the tea has cooled, use it as a final rinse after shampooing your hair. This helps balance the scalp's natural oils.
For oily hair, use this tea 1-2 times a week. Store any leftover tea in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Natural product communications", Artemisia absinthium teas for oily hair may be beneficial due to the presence of neryl isovalerate, geranyl isobutanoate, and carvacrol in its essential oil, which have been shown to have free radical scavenging and anti-lipid peroxidation activities.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Artemisia absinthium teas can be used to treat oily hair ailments, but possible side effects may include skin irritation, allergic reactions, and an increased risk of photosensitivity, which can lead to sunburn or other skin damage. To use Artemisia absinthium teas safely, it is essential to start with small amounts, gradually increase the dosage, and avoid using them for extended periods, as prolonged use can lead to an imbalance of essential oils in the scalp.
preparation_usage: Wash your hair with a gentle shampoo as usual.
Mix 2 tablespoons of Artemisia absinthium tea with 1 cup of water.
Use the tea mixture as a final rinse after shampooing.
Repeat the tea rinse once a week for 2-3 months.
Monitor your hair for improvements in 6-8 weeks.

preparations.herb_desc: Camellia sinensis teas have been traditionally used as a natural remedy to treat oily hair, thanks to their astringent and antiseptic properties. These herbal preparations help to balance the scalp's pH, reducing excess oil production and preventing clogged pores, which in turn minimizes the appearance of oily hair. The bioactive constituents present in Camellia sinensis teas, such as catechins and theaflavins, have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that contribute to its hair-balancing properties. Regular use of Camellia sinensis teas can help to reduce dandruff, itchiness, and flakiness associated with oily hair, promoting a healthy and balanced scalp.
preparation_procedure: Boil 1 cup of fresh water in a kettle or pot.
Measure 1 teaspoon of Camellia sinensis leaves (green, black, or white tea) and place them in a tea infuser or strainer.
Pour the boiling water over the tea leaves in the infuser or strainer.
Steep the tea for 3-5 minutes, then remove the leaves.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 1-2 times a week to help balance oil production in the scalp.
preparations.preparation_study: According to International journal of molecular sciences, Camellia sinensis teas may have potential beneficial features in repairing or recovering from hair follicle disruption due to its flavonoids, although there is no specific mention of its effect on oily hair.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Camellia sinensis teas can be effective in treating oily hair, but possible side effects may include skin irritation, allergic reactions, and excessive dryness of the scalp and hair. To minimize potential side effects, it is recommended to start with a low dose, monitor your body's response, and avoid using the tea on damaged or sensitive hair, as well as to keep the tea away from open wounds or cuts on the scalp.

The herbal preparation helps to treat oily hair by reducing sebum production, balancing the pH of the scalp, and promoting a healthy scalp environment. The bioactive constituents of Urtica dioica, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which contribute to its ability to reduce inflammation and prevent hair loss. The benefits of using Urtica dioica teas to treat oily hair include a reduction in greasiness, improved scalp health, and a decrease in hair loss, making it a natural and effective solution for those struggling with oily hair.

preparations.herb_desc: Aloe barbadensis teas have been traditionally used to treat oily hair ailments due to their unique properties that help to balance the scalp's natural oil production. The anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial growth on the scalp, thereby controlling the excess oil production. The bioactive constituents present in Aloe barbadensis teas, such as aloin, aloe-emodin, and vitamins A, C, and E, work synergistically to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing oiliness and promoting a healthy hair growth. Regular consumption of Aloe barbadensis teas can help to treat oily hair ailments by promoting a balanced scalp environment, reducing oil production, and improving overall hair health.

Cut the Aloe leaves into small pieces and place them in a saucepan.
Pour the cup of water over the Aloe pieces and heat the mixture on low heat.
Let it simmer for 10-15 minutes, then turn off the heat and let it cool.
Strain the mixture and drink 1/2 cup of the Aloe tea 2-3 times a week to help balance oily hair.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Current topics in medicinal chemistry, Aloe barbadensis teas for oily hair is not specifically mentioned in the study, but it is mentioned that Aloe vera has been reported to demonstrate anti-dandruff activity by disrupting microbial growth associated with dandruff formation.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Aloe barbadensis teas can be used to treat oily hair ailments, but possible side effects may include stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. When using Aloe barbadensis teas to treat oily hair, it's essential to take precautions such as using it in moderation, avoiding it on damaged or irritated scalp, and monitoring your body's reaction to prevent any adverse effects.

preparations.herb_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, an herbal preparation derived from the root of the licorice plant, has been traditionally used to treat oily hair. The antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties of this tea help to treat oily hair by reducing the growth of bacteria and calming irritated scalp, thereby regulating oil production. Glycyrrhiza glabra teas contain bioactive constituents such as glycyrrhizin, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic effects, including its ability to inhibit 5-alpha-reductase, an enzyme that converts testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone linked to oily hair. The benefits of using Glycyrrhiza glabra teas for treating oily hair include reduced oil production, a balanced scalp pH, and improved overall hair health, making it a popular natural remedy for this common hair condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of fresh or dried Glycyrrhiza glabra roots, 2 cups of water, and a tea infuser.
Clean and chop the Glycyrrhiza glabra roots into small pieces.
Boil 2 cups of water in a saucepan. Add 1 cup of chopped roots and let it boil for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture using a tea infuser or a fine-mesh sieve into a cup. Discard the solids.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea 2-3 times a week to help reduce oiliness in your hair. Store the tea in the fridge for up to 3 days.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can be used to treat oily hair due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to balance the scalp's natural oils and reduce dandruff. However, prolonged consumption of Glycyrrhiza glabra teas may lead to side effects such as water retention, high blood pressure, and low potassium levels, and it is essential to use it in moderation, limiting the intake to 1-2 cups per week and avoiding it altogether during pregnancy and breastfeeding.

preparations.herb_desc: Thymus serpyllum teas, also known as wild thyme tea, have been traditionally used to treat oily hair ailments due to their astringent and antiseptic properties. The tea's ability to balance the scalp's natural pH and reduce sebum production helps to treat oily hair, making it a popular natural remedy among those suffering from this condition. The bioactive constituents, such as thymol and carvacrol, in Thymus serpyllum teas have antimicrobial properties that help to control the growth of bacteria and fungi on the scalp, contributing to the reduction of oily hair. Regular consumption of Thymus serpyllum teas can help to regulate the scalp's oil production, leading to healthier and more balanced hair.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Thymus serpyllum leaves or 1 teaspoon of dried leaves.
Boil 1 cup of water and let it cool for 5 minutes.
Add the Thymus serpyllum leaves to the cooled water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the leaves. Add 1 tablespoon of lemon juice and 1 tablespoon of honey to taste.
Use the tea as a final rinse after shampooing to help balance oil production in oily hair.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Thymus serpyllum teas can be used to treat oily hair, but it may cause side effects such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and gastrointestinal upset in some individuals, particularly when consumed in large quantities or for extended periods. To minimize potential risks, it is essential to start with small amounts, monitor your body's response, and adjust the usage accordingly, also being cautious of interactions with other medications or substances that may exacerbate the side effects.

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, also known as ginger tea, have been traditionally used to treat oily hair, a common scalp condition characterized by excessive oil production. The properties of ginger tea that help in treating oily hair include its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which help to soothe the scalp and reduce oil production. The bioactive constituents of ginger tea, such as gingerol and shogaol, help to inhibit the activity of 5-alpha-reductase, an enzyme responsible for converting testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that contributes to oily hair. The benefits of ginger tea in treating oily hair include a reduction in oil production, improved scalp health, and a decrease in dandruff and itchiness, making it a natural and effective remedy for this common scalp condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Zingiber officinale (ginger) root.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add the ginger root to the boiling water and reduce heat.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain it into a cup.
Drink the ginger tea as is or add honey to taste.
preparations.preparation_study: According to International journal of preventive medicine, Zingiber officinale teas have been identified as a potential herbal remedy for hirsutism, although the study specifically focused on its potential benefits as a complementary approach to treat excessive hair growth, not oily hair.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can be used to treat oily hair ailments, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and dizziness due to its high ginger content. Precautions should be taken when using Zingiber officinale teas, including starting with small amounts to gauge tolerance, avoiding consumption before or after surgery, and not using it if you have any allergies or are pregnant or breastfeeding.

preparations.herb_desc: Silybum marianum teas have been traditionally used to treat oily hair ailments due to their astringent and antiseptic properties that help to regulate oil production in the scalp. The bioactive constituents present in these teas, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, help to reduce inflammation and soothe the scalp, thereby reducing oiliness. The antifungal properties of Silybum marianum teas also inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi that can contribute to oily hair and scalp conditions. By using Silybum marianum teas as a natural remedy, individuals can benefit from a reduction in oil production, improved scalp health, and a balanced hair condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Silybum marianum flowers and 2 cups of water. Clean and dry the flowers.
Place the flowers in a saucepan and add the water. Bring to a boil over medium heat.
Reduce heat to low and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes. Strain the mixture.
Let the tea cool. Use 2-3 tablespoons of the cooled tea as a final rinse after shampooing your hair.
Leave the tea on your hair for 5-10 minutes before rinsing with cold water. Repeat once a week for best results.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Silybum marianum teas can be used to treat oily hair ailments, but possible side effects include digestive issues, allergic reactions, and interactions with certain medications. When using Silybum marianum teas, precautions should be taken to avoid consuming excessive amounts, which can lead to stomach upset, and to monitor blood pressure and liver function, especially if taken in combination with other supplements.

preparations.herb_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas are often used as a natural remedy to treat oily hair, a common problem faced by many individuals. The properties of this herbal preparation help to treat oily hair by balancing the scalp's natural oil production, reducing inflammation, and promoting healthy hair growth. The bioactive constituents of Ginkgo biloba teas, including flavonoids, terpenoids, and bilobalide, work together to inhibit the production of sebum, the oily substance produced by the scalp, thereby reducing oiliness and promoting a healthier scalp environment. The benefits of using Ginkgo biloba teas to treat oily hair include a reduction in oil production, improved scalp health, and a decrease in the occurrence of dandruff and other scalp irritations.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Ginkgo biloba leaves.
Measure 1/2 cup of boiling water and pour it over the Ginkgo biloba leaves.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup and discard the leaves.
Drink the tea once a day to help reduce oiliness in your hair.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ginkgo biloba teas can be used to treat oily hair, but it may also cause side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset due to its blood-thinning properties, which can interact with other medications. To use Ginkgo biloba teas safely, take the recommended dosage and avoid consuming it with other medications, especially blood thinners and antidepressants, and also be cautious when driving or operating heavy machinery due to the risk of dizziness.
